title: Get Ready for Takeoff With Uber and Joby
text: Joby and Uber unveiled Uber Air powered by Joby, enabling customers to book Joby's electric air taxis through the Uber app. The service will debut in Dubai later in 2026, with plans expanding to New York, Los Angeles, the UK, and Japan. Riders can access the service with a simple tap in the Uber app. The system integrates ground transportation, offering one-tap booking that seamlessly connects and books every leg of the journey. Joby's air taxi accommodates up to four passengers and features six tilting propellers enabling vertical takeoff. The aircraft reaches speeds up to 200 mph with a 100-mile range per charge and operates with an acoustic profile that is quiet enough to blend into the ambient sounds of a city's streets. Before U.S. commercial operations commence, the FAA requires extensive testing and certification. Joby has completed over 50,000 miles of flight tests and begun the final certification stage. The companies have collaborated since 2019. In 2021, Joby acquired Uber's Elevate division, which had developed tools for urban air mobility market analysis and multi-modal operations. The partnership leverages Uber's massive user base for demand generation.
